mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and expanding economic activity, which directly supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation of employment growth and equity performance
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for S&P 500 are negative or flat (within +/- 0.1%) in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth is positive, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates significantly, it signals an overheating economy, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which increases discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations over the longer term.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where the CPIAUCSL trigger condition is met, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a deteriorating labor market and weakening consumer demand, leading to reduced corporate profits and a decline in S&P 500 forward returns as economic recession fears mount.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month simple moving average rises by > 0.5 percentage points from its 6-month low
evidence_refs: fact:unemployment as a lagging indicator of recession
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 35% of periods following the trigger condition, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with an accommodative federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS below 2%) indicates strong consumer spending power supported by low borrowing costs, creating a highly favorable environment for corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with strong demand
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where both conditions are met, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) alongside a low unemployment rate (UNRATE below 5%) signifies an economy operating at high capacity with efficient resource utilization, leading to strong corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where both INDPRO growth and low UNRATE conditions are met, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of confirmed economic expansion (GDPC1 annualized growth > 2%), strong housing starts (HOUST) act as a leading indicator of future economic activity and consumer confidence, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns as investment and consumption increase.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.0% AND HOUST 3-month average > 1200 (thousands of units)
evidence_refs: fact:housing as a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of periods where both GDP growth and housing starts conditions are met, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS above 3.0%), any further increase in the Federal Funds Rate signals a more restrictive policy stance, which immediately raises borrowing costs, dampens economic activity, and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.25 percentage points in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:impact of interest rate hikes on markets
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the FEDFUNDS trigger condition is met within a tightening regime, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing and mining output, reflecting healthy business investment and demand, which supports corporate earnings and drives short-term positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial output as a proxy for economic health
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward returns for S&P 500 are negative or flat (within +/- 0.1%) in more than 55% of months where INDPRO growth is positive, the hypothesis is rejected.
